PREGUNTAS:
1. Could you elucidate how the Disciplinary and Grievance Policy interfaces with existing Ciklum policies, particularly in instances where there is potential overlap, and specify which policies take precedence?

2. In the context of Ciklum IT Spain SLU, how are disciplinary actions defined and what procedures are established to ensure that these procedures are both comprehensive and in compliance with Spanish legislation?

3. How does the policy address the potential for amendments or withdrawals, and what legal circumstances might necessitate such changes to the Disciplinary and Grievance Policy?
---
Disciplinary and Grievance Policy | Spain
Confidential
Date: 
2023
 May 1st, 
Process owner:  
 
Head of HR Operations
Individuals who have cooperation relations with Ciklum LLC Entities (hereinafter 
Intended audience: 
also referred to as “the Company”).
Contents
1. About This Policy
2. Definitions
3. Policy Statement
4. Types of misconduct
5. Disciplinary procedure
6. Investigations
7. Informing the Ciklumer of the problem
8. Disciplinary hearing
9. Disciplinary Actions (Sanctions)
10. Suspension
11. Grievance Matters
12. Grievance hearing
13. Appeals on disciplinary and grievance matters
14. Record keeping
1. About This Policy
1.1 This Disciplinary and Grievance Policy (hereinafter – the “Policy”) is the addendum to Ciklum Global Dignity and Respect Policy and sets out 
principles for handling disciplinary and grievance situations in Ciklumer’s workplace. It is intended to provide standards of managing Ciklumers’ 
misconduct and grievances. 
1.2 This document is directly applicable and addressed to all Ciklum IT Spain SLU employees, who shall be expected to apply these principles and 
guidelines in compliance with effective legal requirements and regulations. 
1.3 This Policy is not intended to be contractual in nature and is not implied or otherwise incorporated into any employment contract or contract for 
services provision. The Company may from time to time, at its discretion, amend or withdraw the Policy if it is required by law.
1.4 All Ciklumers should be made aware of (and acknowledge familiarization with) the provisions of this Policy.
1.5 This Policy is made in line with the other existing policies in Ciklum.
1.6 Wherever a case can be regulated by multiple policies, the more detailed applicable policy will prevail. In case of discrepancy, Worker Statute and 
Collective Agreement shall prevail over any policy.
2. Definitions
With reference to the general legal framework and for the purposes of this Policy, the terms mentioned herein shall have the following meanings: 
Ciklum IT Spain SLU (hereinafter also referred to as a “Company” or “Ciklum”) – a legal entity duly established and operating under the laws of 
the Spanish legislation. 
Conduct - Ciklumer’s behavior in the framework of cooperation with Ciklum Group Company in any form.
Disciplinary Action – a formal action towards a Ciklumer.
Disciplinary Procedure - a procedure for the Company to follow to deal with cases of a Ciklumer’s misconduct. 
Ciklumer - the collective reference for employees, permanent or temporary, full or part time, of any Ciklum legal entity, or any of its affiliates or 
subsidiaries, as well as for other individuals performing work for or on behalf of Ciklum.  
Grievance - a problem or concern that a Ciklumer has about their work, working conditions or cooperation with the Company or Ciklumer(s).
Grievance Procedure - a procedure for the Company to use to consider Ciklumers’ grievances.
Gross Misconduct - acts (or failure to act) that entail negative consequences, sufficient to justify a possible dismissal or termination of employment of 
a Ciklumer.
Hearing officer – Ciklum appointed officer who is holding or participating in the Disciplinary hearing procedure.

PREGUNTAS:
1. ¿Cuál es el procedimiento formal que debe seguir un Ciklumer si no logra resolver de manera informal un problema con su gerente y socio de recursos humanos?
   
2. ¿En qué casos se puede aplicar una destitución sin preaviso a un Ciklumer por motivo de incumplimiento?

3. ¿Qué derechos tienen los Ciklumers durante una audiencia disciplinaria según las políticas de la compañía?

Investigating Officer – Ciklum appointed officer who holds the investigation procedure prior to Disciplinary hearing;
Natural Justice - refers to the basic fundamental principles of fair treatment. These principles include the duty to give someone a fair hearing; the 
duty to ensure that the matter is resolved by someone who is impartial; and the duty to allow an appeal against a decision.
Reasonable Adjustments or Reasonable Accommodations - a way of preventing discrimination against Ciklumers with disabilities by making 
changes to ensure that they are not at a disadvantage. 
Disciplinary Action (Sanction) - a punishment imposed on an Ciklumer as a result of (Minor, Gross or Critical) Misconduct
Summary Dismissal or Involuntary Termination – a justifiable dismissal for critical misconduct occurrence without notice.
3. Policy Statement
3.1 This Policy envisages formal procedure for investigation and settlement of both Disciplinary and Grievance cases taking place within Ciklum.
3.2 Before a grievance is raised Ciklumers are asked to exhaust a ‘normal’/ ‘informal’ resolution via their manager and HR Partner.
3.3 When an informal action has proven ineffective or in any of serious instances of misconduct, formal steps outlined in this Policy should be 
followed. 
3.4 The Company is committed to adhering to the following principles in the course of settlement of disciplinary and grievance matters:
3.4.1 Informal action will be considered, where appropriate, to resolve problems.
3.4.2 No disciplinary action will be taken against a Ciklumer until the case has been fully investigated.
3.4.3 For formal action the Ciklumer will be advised of the nature of the complaint against him or her and will be given the opportunity to state 
his or her case before any decision is made at a Disciplinary hearing. Ciklumers will be provided, where appropriate, with written copies of 
evidence and relevant witness statements in advance of a Disciplinary hearing. 
3.4.4 At all stages of the Disciplinary hearing procedure the Ciklumer will have the right to be accompanied by another Ciklumer. No Ciklumer 
will be dismissed and no cooperation will be terminated with a Ciklumer for the first breach of discipline except in the case of critical 
misconduct, when the penalty will be dismissal or termination of cooperation without notice or payment in lieu of notice. 
3.4.5 A Ciklumer will have the right to appeal against any disciplinary action in accordance with this Policy. 
3.4.6 The Policy may be implemented at any stage if the Ciklumer’s alleged misconduct warrants this.
4. Types of misconduct
The Company considers potentially fair to dismiss a Ciklumer or to terminate cooperation with a Ciklumer for misconduct, which may be either a single 
act of misconduct or a series of acts that refer to a significant breach of expected behavioral standards, Ciklum Code of Conduct or repeated 
Misconduct (offenses). The misconduct/ faults committed by employees in the service of the Company will be classified according to their importance, 
recidivism and intention, in minor, gross and very serious/ critical, in accordance with the provisions of the applicable Collective Agreement and the 
regulations of the labor legal system in Spain
4.1 Minor Misconduct
4.1.1 Misconduct in some instances may be resolved informally with a Ciklumer’s Line manager, local HR Professional or responsible HR Partner
4.1.2 Informal action may take a form of a discussion, where the Line Manager and local HR Professional or responsible HR Partner of the Company 
talk to the Ciklumer and ask her/him to explain their actions, invite them to provide information about any relevant personal details or health reasons 
that may have affected their actions and reminds them of proper standards of behavior.
4.1.3 The following non-exhaustive list provides examples of offenses that are normally regarded as a misconduct: 
Poor timekeeping or failure to follow flexible hours arrangements (up to 3 times a month).
Unexcused absence from work up to one day in a month
Failure to carry out a reasonable management instruction which however did not lead to major consequences.
Misuse of Company assets and systems handed out/granted access to a Ciklumer.
Failure to notify sick absence timely as required by law for ‘baja’ medical leave and promptly – for undocumented sick leave.
Being unfit to come to work or to perform services due to effects of alcohol/drugs (occasionally). 
Being rude to Ciklumers and/or to the Company customers
Smoking in a no smoking area or minor breach of any health and safety regulations as might be  in place for Ciklumers.
Repetition of a misconduct which the Ciklumer has already been warned about either formally or informally in the last 60 calendar days. 
Failure to comply with operating instructions or safety, health and environment precautions.
Lack of effort/co-operation with Ciklumers or Clients.
Insubordination, failure to follow reasonable instructions with serious consequences, for example, damage to Ciklum property or disruption to 
service delivery of Ciklum.
Exceeding level of authority with negative impact on the business.
Inadvertent breach of Ciklum confidentiality rules however communicated or assumed.

PREGUNTAS:
1. ¿Cómo define el documento el concepto de "Falta Grave" y qué ejemplos se brindan como faltas que son generalmente consideradas como tal?

2. ¿Cuál es el procedimiento disciplinario que la empresa debe seguir según el documento cuando un empleado comete una falta, y cuáles son las etapas claves incluidas en este proceso?

3. ¿Qué acciones están identificadas como "Faltas Muy Graves/Criticas" que podrían deteriorar fundamentalmente la confianza entre el empleado y la empresa, y cómo se diferencia esto de la "Falta Grave"?

Failure to notify the loss of Company equipment, Company documents or information/data as a matter of urgency to the Line Manager or 
failure to immediately notify the police – where the loss may have been in public.
Offensive personal behavior, for example abuse of a Ciklumer (verbally or in writing including but not limited to by email or social media).
Vexatious or malicious complaints and grievances.
Bullying, harassment and other acts of discrimination.
Minor breach of Company’s policies and procedures, collective agreement (Article 24) or workers statutory (Article 54)
4.2 Gross Misconduct 
4.2.1 Gross misconduct is either deliberate wrongdoing or gross negligence by Ciklumer, which is so serious that it fundamentally undermines the 
relationship of trust and confidence between the Ciklumer and the Company.
4.2.2 The following non-exhaustive list provides examples of offenses that are normally regarded as Gross Misconduct: 
Unexcused absence from work up to two days in a month.
 Simulating illness or accident.
Simulating the presence of another worker, using their laptop, signature or means of control.
Recidivism in Misconduct, except for punctuality, even if they are of a different nature, within a quarter.
Leaving work without just cause.
Negligence at work when it causes serious damage.
Gross breach of Company’s policies and procedures, collective agreement (Article 24) or workers statutory (Article 54)
4.3 Very serious/ Critical Misconduct 
4.3.1 Very serious/ Critical Misconduct is either deliberate wrongdoing or very critical negligence by Ciklumer, which is so serious that it fundamentally 
undermines the relationship of trust and confidence between the Ciklumer and the Company. 
Absence from work for more than two days a month without just cause
Fraud, disloyalty and breach of trust in the work entrusted to them.
Theft from the rest of the staff and from the company or from any person inside or outside the company.
Any kind of harassment: racial or ethnic, religion or belief, disability, age, sexual orientation…
The proven simulation of illness
Disabling, destroying or causing damage to assets, equipments, installations, buildings, belongings and departments of the company
To have been convicted by the Court of Justice for crimes of theft, robbery, fraud and embezzlement, committed outside the company, which 
may cause mistrust towards the perpetrator.
Continuous lack of workplace and personal cleanliness which gives rise to justified complaints from colleagues
Attending work under the influence of alcohol or drugs or in possession of illegal drugs.
Conduct likely to seriously offend customers, suppliers, visitors of the Company or Ciklumers and that detracts from the Company’s good 
name and reputation including swearing or aggressive behavior. 
Fighting, extreme forms of bullying, harassment, vilification, victimization and/or other act of discrimination or actual or threatened assault on 
another person.
Reckless, willful or deliberate damage to the Company’s property or name.
Being concerned or involved in action that is damaging or anti-competitive to the business of the Company (e.g. deliberate sharing of smart-
cards, passwords or other access control devices that provide access to customer, Ciklum premises, property and/or systems and/or to 
Ciklumer or other sensitive information).
Willful or negligent behavior causing unacceptable loss and damage to the Company.
Deliberately accessing internet sites containing pornographic, offensive or obscene material using Ciklum property or in Ciklum premises 
during work hours.
Serious act of insubordination.
Bringing the Company into serious disrepute.
Serious breach of health and safety rules.
Serious breach of confidence.
Recidivism in gross misconduct, even if of a different nature, within the same quarter.
Serious breach of Company’s policies and procedures, collective agreement (Article 24) or workers statutory (Article 54). 
5. Disciplinary procedure
With reference to the general principles outlined in Article 3 above, the Company recognizes the Ciklumer’s right to fair and reasonable disciplinary 
procedure that should include:
5.1 Initial assessment, including levels of seriousness of misconduct.
5.2 Investigation.
5.3 Notification of the Ciklumer about the problem.
5.4 Disciplinary hearing.
5.5 Decision on appropriate action.
5.6 Appeal.

PREGUNTAS:
1. ¿Cuáles son los posibles resultados que puede esperar un empleado después de una audiencia disciplinaria en caso de que se considere que han cometido una falta leve, falta grave o falta muy seria/ crítica, según los procedimientos actuales de la compañía?

2. Si un empleado no puede asistir a la reunión de investigación programada por razones de salud, ¿cuál es el proceso que la compañía seguirá para tomar una decisión basada en la evidencia disponible?

3. Durante una audiencia disciplinaria, ¿qué derechos tiene un empleado en cuanto a su defensa, presentación de pruebas y solicitud de un aplazamiento para realizar más investigaciones sobre los puntos nuevos planteados?
---
1.  
2.

1.

6. Investigations
6.1 The purpose of an investigation is for the Company to establish a fair and balanced view of the facts relating to any disciplinary allegations against 
the Ciklumer, before deciding whether to proceed with a disciplinary hearing. 
6.2 The level of detail of investigation required will depend on the nature of allegations and will vary from case to case. It may involve interviewing and 
taking statements from the Ciklumer and any witnesses, and/or reviewing relevant documents. 
6.3 The Company undertakes to appoint an Investigating Officer to carry out the investigation.
6.4 Investigative interviews are held solely for the purpose of fact-finding and no decision on disciplinary action will be taken until after a disciplinary 
hearing has been held. 
6.5 The Ciklumer must cooperate fully and promptly with any investigation. This will include informing the Company of the names of any relevant 
witnesses, disclosing any relevant documents and attending investigative interviews, if required.
6.6 If the Ciklumer cannot attend the investigation meeting, he/she should inform the Investigating Officer immediately, so that an alternative time can 
be arranged. The Ciklumer must make every effort to attend the meeting, and failure to attend without good reason may be treated as misconduct in 
itself. If the Ciklumer fails to attend without good reason or is persistently unable to do so (for example for health reasons), the Company may have to 
reach its conclusions based on the available evidence.
7. Informing the Ciklumer of the problem
7.1 If there is no case to answer based on the results of the investigation, the Investigating Officer must write (email is acceptable means) to the 
Ciklumer to confirm the decision and say that no further disciplinary action is taken. A copy of the report and witness statements should be enclosed 
(files attached to an email are sufficient).
7.2 If following an investigation, it is considered that there is a case to answer, the Ciklumer will be invited to a disciplinary hearing. 
7.3 The Ciklumer should be given at least 48 hours’ notice in writing (an email is an acceptable means of written notification) about disciplinary 
hearing, which should set out the allegations and possible consequences.
7.4 The Ciklumer should be provided with copies of evidence (files sent from Ciklum email are sufficient) to be considered in advance of the 
disciplinary hearing.
8. Disciplinary hearing
8.1 The disciplinary hearing will be held without unreasonable delay (finalized within 20 calendar days) 
8.2 The Ciklumer has the right to be accompanied by a Ciklumer or another representative. The Ciklumer shall notify the Company about an 
accompanying person at least 24 hours before the date of disciplinary hearing, specifying such person’s full name and relation to the Ciklumer. 
8.3 At the hearing, a Hearing officer appointed by the Company will explain the case against the Ciklumer and review the evidence. The Ciklumer will 
be allowed to respond to the allegations, ask questions, present evidence and request an adjournment if required. The disciplinary hearing may be 
adjourned if it is necessary to carry out any further investigations (e.g. re-interviewing witnesses in light of any new points raised) and also for the Line 
Manager and the Hearing officer to consider what they have heard and to make their decision.
8.4 The Hearing officer will confirm the outcome at the end of the hearing in writing (email is sufficient) to the Ciklumer within five (5) business days of 
the hearing, advising them of the right of appeal.
9. Disciplinary Actions (Sanctions)
9.1 In the course of the disciplinary hearing the Company is committed to ensuring the reasonableness of its response to the circumstances of the 
case.
9.2 Based on the results of the disciplinary hearing one of the following disciplinary actions (sanctions) may be taken:
9.2.1 Minor Misconduct 
Verbal warning.
Written warning.
One day of suspension from employment and payment.
9.2.2 Gross Misconduct 
Up to ten days of suspension from employment and payment.
Disqualification, for a period not exceeding one year, from promotion to the next higher work category.
9.2.3 Very serious/ Critical Misconduct

PREGUNTAS:
1. ¿Qué procedimientos debe seguir un Ciklumer si desea apelar una decisión de despido o una sanción inferior al despido según el fragmento presentado?

2. En caso de que un Ciklumer presente una queja o agravio durante un procedimiento disciplinario, ¿cómo se maneja la situación según las políticas establecidas en el documento?

3. ¿Cuáles son las condiciones bajo las cuales una suspensión de trabajo y pago puede ser implementada durante un proceso disciplinario y cómo afecta esto a los derechos del empleado involucrado?
---
1.  
2.  
3.  
4.  
Temporary or permanent loss of professional category.
Up to two months of suspension from employment and payment.
Disqualification for two years or permanent disqualification to change to another work category.
Dismissal. Critical misconduct of the Ciklumer entitles the Company to dismiss the Ciklumer without notice (summary dismissal/involuntary 
termination) or to terminate the contract without notice or without pay in lieu of notice. 
9.3 Written Warning 
9.3.1 Written Warning should set our details of the complaint, the improvement required and the timescale. It will also warn that failure to improve may 
lead to dismissal (or some other action short of dismissal) or to the termination of cooperation and will refer to the right of appeal. A copy of this written 
warning will be kept by local HR Professional or representative or responsible People Partner / HR Business Partner but will be disregarded for 
disciplinary purposes after 2 (two) months subject to achieving and sustaining satisfactory conduct.
9.4 Action Short of Dismissal or Dismissal:
9.4.1 If there is still further misconduct or failure to improve conduct, the final step or some other action short of dismissal such as demotion or 
disciplinary suspension or transfer (as allowed in Ciklumer’s Employment Agreement). 
9.4.2 If some sanction short of dismissal or termination of cooperation is imposed, the Ciklumer will receive details of the complaint, will be warned 
that dismissal could result if there is no satisfactory improvement, and will be advised of the right of appeal. A copy of the written warning will be kept 
by local HR Professional or responsible HR Partner but will be disregarded for disciplinary purposes after 2 (two) months subject to achievement and 
sustainment of satisfactory conduct or performance.
9.4.3 Dismissal decisions can only be taken by the appropriate Line Manager, and the Ciklumer should be provided in writing with reasons for 
dismissal, the date on which the employment will terminate, and the right of appeal.
9.4.4 If based on the results of disciplinary hearing a decision to dismiss the Ciklumer for misconduct is taken, the Company is committed to ensuring 
the following:
9.4.5 At the time of the dismissal, it genuinely believed the Ciklumer to be guilty of misconduct.
9.4.6 It had reasonable grounds for believing that the Ciklumer was guilty of that misconduct.
9.4.7 At the time that it formed that belief on those grounds, it carried out as much investigation as was reasonable in the circumstances.
9.4.8 Dismissal fell within the range of reasonable responses available to the Company in the circumstances. 
10. Suspension
10.1 When a disciplinary issue is being looked into, a Ciklumer may be suspended from work and payment. 
10.2 Suspension may be appropriate immediately following an incident or later at any stage in the disciplinary procedure.
10.3 During the period of suspension, the Ciklumer keeps his/her employment rights, including, but not limited to the right to receive a salary at full pay 
rate. (excluding the suspension days)
10.4 Where Ciklumer raises a grievance during a disciplinary process, the disciplinary process may be temporarily suspended in order to deal with the 
grievance. Where the grievance and disciplinary cases are related, it may be appropriate to deal with both issues concurrently.
11. Grievance Matters 
11.1 If the Ciklumer has a grievance or complaint to do with the work with Ciklumers and/or with Ciklum, he/she is encouraged to start by talking it 
over with the Line Manager or responsible HR Partner. Where a solution may be found informally, an informal procedure should be followed.
11.2 If the matter is serious and/or the Ciklumer wishes to raise the matter formally, he/she should set out the grievance in writing to the Line Manager 
or responsible HR Partner. The grievance complaint should stick to the facts and avoid language that is insulting or abusive. 
11.3 Where the Ciklumer’s grievance is against his/her Line Manager and the Ciklumer feels unable to approach him or her, a Senior Manager or 
responsible HR Partner should be addressed or through our Speak Up tool.
12. Grievance hearing
12.1 Line Manager, local HR professional or responsible HR Partner should arrange a meeting to discuss Ciklumer’s grievance within the term of five 
(5) business days as for the date the grievance was received; the term starts the next business day after the date on which the grievance was 
received. 
12.2 Similar to the original disciplinary hearing, the Ciklumer has the right to be accompanied to the appeal hearing.
12.3 After the meeting, Line Manager or responsible HR Partner should give a decision to the Ciklumer in writing within 5 work days as of the moment 
the meeting was over.

PREGUNTAS:
1. If an employee wants to appeal a disciplinary decision, what is the deadline for submitting the appeal, and what steps must they go through according to the policy?

2. In the disciplinary and grievance process, who is responsible for hearing the appeals and within what timeframe will a final decision be reached after the last appeal hearing?

3. How does the company ensure transparency and maintain written records throughout the disciplinary and grievance proceedings, and are there exceptions to what information might be disclosed to the employee involved?
---
12.4 If the meeting has ended on the last work day of the work week or before the public holiday, the 24 hours term expires at the relevant hour of the 
first work day after the public holiday or weekend.
13. Appeals on disciplinary and grievance matters
13.1 If a Ciklumer is unhappy with any decision made by the Company in relation to the disciplinary or grievance matter, the Ciklumer should follow 
formal procedures of appeal.
13.2 A Ciklumer who wishes to appeal against a disciplinary or grievance decision must do so within five (5) business days from the day of receipt of 
the decision. 
13.3 The Ciklumer’s appeal shall be submitted in writing to Hearing Officer and state reasons for making an appeal.
13.4 The Senior Manager or responsible HR Partner will appoint a separate hearing to hear the appeal and decide the case as impartial as possible. 
At the appeal, any disciplinary penalty imposed will be reviewed.
13.5 Similar to the original disciplinary hearing, the Ciklumer has the right to be accompanied to the appeal hearing.
13.6 The Senior Manager or responsible HR Partner will hear all appeals and issue a final decision within (five) 5 business days following the day of 
the last appeal hearing.
14. Record keeping
14.1 The Company will keep written records of every disciplinary and grievance case considered. 
14.2 Record keeping should comprise the various stages of the procedure, including: the complaint; the Ciklumer's defense; notes taken during the 
hearings and appeals, findings made; actions taken and reasons for them; whether there were appeals and their outcome; any grievances raised 
during the procedure; any subsequent developments; notes of any formal and informal meetings.
14.3 Copies of meeting records should be given to the Ciklumer including copies of any formal and informal meetings that may have been taken. In 
some circumstances, such as to protect a witness, the Company might be justified in withholding some information.
